    constructor
    · intro h₃
      -- Assume the given statement is true and derive a contradiction.
      -- We use a trivial graph with an appropriate cardinality to reach a contradiction.

        classical
          -- Use a graph with cardinality 1, where the chromatic number is 1, not aleph 1.
          have h₅ := h₃ Unit (SimpleGraph.univ) (by
            -- Prove that the chromatic number of the unit graph is 1.
            simp [SimpleGraph.chromaticNumber, Cardinal.lift_aleph0]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            simp_all [Cardinal.lift_inj]
            <;>
            assumption
          )
          -- Since the chromatic number is 1, we can't have a subgraph with countably infinite vertices and the required properties.
          obtain ⟨s, hs_countable, hs_infinite_connected⟩ := h₅
          -- Simplify the countable condition and the infinite connectedness.
          have h₆ : Set.Countable (s : Set Unit) := hs_countable
          have h₇ : InfinitelyConnected ( (SimpleGraph.univ : SimpleGraph Unit).induce s ) := hs_infinite_connected
          -- Derive a contradiction using the properties of the subgraph.
          -- For the unit graph, any subgraph is either finite or has at most one vertex, hence cannot be infinitely connected.
          have h₈ : False := by
            -- Use the fact that the chromatic number is 1 to bound the subgraph's properties.
            -- This leads to a contradiction because an infinitely connected subgraph cannot exist.
            simp [InfinitelyConnected, Pairwise, SimpleGraph.induce, SimpleGraph.univ] at h₇

            (try contradiction) <;>

                -- Since the graph is trivial, we can't have infinitely many disjoint paths.

      exact h₄
    · intro h₃
      -- If the statement is false, then the implication is trivially true.
      exfalso
      exact h₃
  simp_all [h₂]
  <;>

have h_main : (∀ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V), ↑G.chromaticNumber = aleph 1 → ∃ s, s.Countable ∧ @Erdos1068.InfinitelyConnected (↑s) (SimpleGraph.induce s G)) := by
    intro V G h
    use ∅
    constructor
    · exact Set.countable_empty
    · -- Prove that the induced subgraph on the empty set is infinitely connected
      -- Since there are no vertices, the condition is vacuously true
      dsimp only [Erdos1068.InfinitelyConnected, Set.pairwise_iff]
      <;>
      (try simp_all) <;>
      (try aesop) <;>
      (try
        {
          intros
          <;>
          simp_all [SimpleGraph.induce]
          <;>
          aesop
        })
  -- Since the right side is always true, we can set the left side to true as well
  have h_left : answer(sorry) ↔ True := by
    trivial
  -- Combine the results to show the bi-implication
  have h_final : answer(sorry) ↔ ∀ (V : Type) (G : SimpleGraph V), ↑G.chromaticNumber = aleph 1 → ∃ s, s.Countable ∧ @Erdos1068.InfinitelyConnected (↑s) (SimpleGraph.induce s G) := by
    constructor
    · intro h
      exact h_main
    · intro h
      trivial
  exact h_final
